title: Sesame brittle
subtitle: Caramelised sugar with toasted sesame seeds
excerpt: Sesame seeds suspended in hard-crack caramel, poured thin onto oiled paper and left to cool into a shard-like brittle. The sesame should be toasted before it goes into the sugar. Makes one slab.
sourceNotes: Sesame brittle is documented across the Middle Eastern and Mediterranean confectionery traditions, where sesame-and-honey brittle (pasteli, simsim, tahini brittle) has been made since antiquity. Mrs Rundell, A New System of Domestic Cookery (1806), Project Gutenberg: sugar brittle made by pouring hot caramel over seeds or nuts and leaving to set thin is documented in the confectionery chapter. The USDA public-domain publications on sesame production and food applications document sesame brittle as a widely distributed confection made from sugar cooked to hard-crack stage with toasted sesame seeds.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps in a sealed tin at room temperature for up to 2 weeks. Keep dry; moisture softens the brittle.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: deep caramel, 155°C; the sugar should be a rich amber before the seeds go in

content.text: Toast the sesame seeds in a dry pan before adding them to the caramel; raw seeds in hot sugar will carry a bitter, grassy note. The caramel should reach 155°C before the seeds go in; the colour at that point is deep amber. Pour it thin for brittle rather than toffee. If hot syrup touches skin, run cold water over it for at least ten minutes and seek medical care if needed.

items.prepNote: toasted in a dry pan until golden

content.text: Line a baking tray with lightly oiled baking paper. Warm the {{sesame-seeds}} in a small pan over low heat while the caramel cooks; they should be warm when they go in. Put the {{caster-sugar}}, {{glucose-syrup}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and 50 ml cold water in a medium saucepan. Stir over low heat until the sugar dissolves. Stop stirring, clip in the sugar thermometer, and raise the heat to medium. Cook without stirring until the caramel reaches 155°C and is a rich amber. Remove from the heat, quickly stir in the warm {{sesame-seeds}}, then pour immediately onto the prepared tray. Tilt the tray to spread the brittle as thinly as possible. Leave to cool and harden for 30 minutes before breaking into shards.
